sentences.text: Honorable UN Secretary General Mr. Ban Ki-moon, respected President of the General Assembly Vuk Jeremic, honorable UN envoy for global education Mr. Gordon Brown.
sentences.translation: 尊敬的联合国秘书长潘基文先生、尊敬的联合国大会主席武克·耶雷米奇先生、尊敬的联合国全球教育特使戈登·布朗先生。

sentences.text: Respected elders and my dear brothers and sisters. Today is a day of great happiness for me.
sentences.translation: 尊敬的长辈们和我亲爱的兄弟姐妹们。今天对我来说是非常快乐的一天。

sentences.text: I am humbled that the United Nations has named this day Malala Day, and it is an honor for me to be here.
sentences.translation: 联合国将这一天命名为马拉拉日，我深感荣幸能来到这里。

sentences.text: Dear brothers and sisters, do remember one thing: Malala Day is not my day. Today is the day of every woman, every boy, and every girl who have raised their voice for their rights.
sentences.translation: 亲爱的兄弟姐妹们，请记住一件事：马拉拉日不是我的日子。今天是每一个为自己权利发声的女性、男孩和女孩的日子。

sentences.text: There are hundreds of human rights activists and social workers who are not only speaking for human rights, but who are struggling to achieve their goals of education, peace, and equality.
sentences.translation: 有数百名人权活动家和社会工作者不仅在为人权发声，而且在为实现教育、和平和平等的目标而奋斗。

sentences.text: Thousands of people have been killed by the terrorists, and millions have been injured. I am just one of them.
sentences.translation: 数千人被恐怖分子杀害，数百万人受伤。我只是其中之一。

sentences.text: So here I stand, one girl among many. I speak not for myself, but for all girls and boys.
sentences.translation: 所以我站在这里，众多女孩中的一个。我不是为自己发言，而是为所有的女孩和男孩。

sentences.text: I raise up my voice, not so that I can shout, but so that those without a voice can be heard.
sentences.translation: 我提高声音，不是为了呐喊，而是为了让那些没有声音的人被听到。

sentences.text: Those who have fought for their rights: their right to live in peace, their right to be treated with dignity, their right to equality of opportunity, their right to be educated.
sentences.translation: 那些为自己权利而战的人：他们和平生活的权利，被有尊严地对待的权利，享有平等机会的权利，接受教育的权利。

sentences.text: Dear friends, on the ninth of October 2012, the Taliban shot me on the left side of my forehead.
sentences.translation: 亲爱的朋友们，2012年10月9日，塔利班在我的左额射了我一枪。

sentences.text: They shot my friends too. They thought that the bullets would silence us. But they failed.
sentences.translation: 他们也射了我的朋友们。他们以为子弹会让我们沉默。但他们失败了。

sentences.text: And then, out of that silence came thousands of voices.
sentences.translation: 然后，从那沉默中传出了数千个声音。

sentences.text: The terrorists thought that they would change our aims and stop our ambitions, but nothing changed in my life except this: weakness, fear, and hopelessness died.
sentences.translation: 恐怖分子以为他们能改变我们的目标并阻止我们的抱负，但我生活中除了一件事什么都没有改变：软弱、恐惧和绝望死了。

sentences.text: Strength, power, and courage were born.
sentences.translation: 力量、能量和勇气诞生了。

sentences.text: I am the same Malala. My ambitions are the same. My hopes are the same. My dreams are the same.
sentences.translation: 我还是同一个马拉拉。我的抱负没变。我的希望没变。我的梦想没变。

sentences.text: Dear sisters and brothers, I am not against anyone. Neither am I here to speak in terms of personal revenge against the Taliban.
sentences.translation: 亲爱的姐妹兄弟们，我不反对任何人。我也不是来对塔利班进行个人报复的。

sentences.text: I am here to speak up for the right of education of every child. I want education for the sons and the daughters of all the extremists.
sentences.translation: 我在这里是为每个孩子的受教育权发声。我希望所有极端分子的儿女都能接受教育。

sentences.text: I do not even hate the Talib who shot me. Even if there is a gun in my hand and he stands in front of me, I would not shoot him.
sentences.translation: 我甚至不恨那个射杀我的塔利班分子。即使我手中有枪，他站在我面前，我也不会射杀他。

sentences.text: This is the compassion that I have learned from Muhammad, the Prophet of mercy, Jesus Christ, and Lord Buddha.
sentences.translation: 这是我从仁慈的先知穆罕默德、耶稣基督和佛陀那里学到的慈悲。

sentences.text: This is the legacy of change that I have inherited from Martin Luther King, Nelson Mandela, and Muhammad Ali Jinnah.
sentences.translation: 这是我从马丁·路德·金、纳尔逊·曼德拉和穆罕默德·阿里·真纳那里继承的变革遗产。

sentences.text: This is the philosophy of nonviolence that I have learned from Gandhi, Bacha Khan, and Mother Teresa.
sentences.translation: 这是我从甘地、巴沙汗和特蕾莎修女那里学到的非暴力哲学。

sentences.text: And this is the forgiveness that I have learned from my mother and father.
sentences.translation: 这是我从父母那里学到的宽恕。

sentences.text: This is what my soul is telling me: be peaceful and love everyone.
sentences.translation: 这是我的灵魂告诉我的：保持和平，爱每一个人。

sentences.text: We call upon all governments to ensure free compulsory education for every child all over the world.
sentences.translation: 我们呼吁所有政府确保全世界每个孩子都能获得免费的义务教育。

sentences.text: We call upon all governments to fight against terrorism and violence, to protect children from brutality and harm.
sentences.translation: 我们呼吁所有政府打击恐怖主义和暴力，保护儿童免受残暴和伤害。

sentences.text: We call upon the developed nations to support the expansion of educational opportunities for girls in the developing world.
sentences.translation: 我们呼吁发达国家支持扩大发展中世界女孩的教育机会。

sentences.text: We call upon all communities to be tolerant, to reject prejudice based on caste, creed, sect, religion, or gender.
sentences.translation: 我们呼吁所有社区要宽容，拒绝基于种姓、信条、教派、宗教或性别的偏见。

sentences.text: To ensure freedom and equality for women so that they can flourish. We cannot all succeed when half of us are held back.
sentences.translation: 确保妇女的自由和平等，使她们能够蓬勃发展。当我们中的一半被阻碍时，我们不可能都成功。

sentences.text: Let us pick up our books and pens. They are our most powerful weapons.
sentences.translation: 让我们拿起书本和笔。它们是我们最强大的武器。

sentences.text: One child, one teacher, one book, and one pen can change the world.
sentences.translation: 一个孩子、一位老师、一本书和一支笔可以改变世界。

sentences.text: Education is the only solution. Education first.
sentences.translation: 教育是唯一的解决方案。教育优先。

sentences.text: We realize the importance of our voice only when we are silenced.
sentences.translation: 只有当我们被噤声时，我们才意识到自己声音的重要性。

sentences.text: The wise saying goes: the pen is mightier than the sword. This is true.
sentences.translation: 有句智慧的话说：笔比剑更有力量。这是真的。

sentences.text: The extremists are afraid of books and pens. The power of education frightens them.
sentences.translation: 极端分子害怕书本和笔。教育的力量让他们恐惧。

sentences.text: They are afraid of women. The power of the voice of women frightens them.
sentences.translation: 他们害怕女性。女性声音的力量让他们恐惧。

sentences.text: We must believe in the power and the strength of our words. Our words can change the world.
sentences.translation: 我们必须相信我们话语的力量和力量。我们的话语可以改变世界。

sentences.text: Because we are all together, united for the cause of education. And we will win through our struggle.
sentences.translation: 因为我们团结在一起，为教育事业而团结。我们将通过我们的斗争取得胜利。

sentences.text: Let us wage a global struggle against illiteracy, poverty, and terrorism, and let us pick up our books and pens.
sentences.translation: 让我们发起一场反对文盲、贫穷和恐怖主义的全球斗争，让我们拿起书本和笔。

sentences.text: It is our duty. So let us wage a glorious struggle against illiteracy and ignorance, because that is how we can build our future.
sentences.translation: 这是我们的责任。让我们发起一场反对文盲和无知的光荣斗争，因为那是我们建设未来的方式。

sentences.text: Dear sisters and brothers, we want schools and education for every child's bright future. We will continue our journey to our destination of peace and education for everyone. Thank you.
sentences.translation: 亲爱的姐妹兄弟们，我们希望每个孩子都有学校和教育，拥有光明的未来。我们将继续为所有人的和平与教育之旅前行。谢谢。

sentences.text: Today we are launching a campaign called HeForShe. I am reaching out to you because I need your help.
sentences.translation: 今天我们发起了一项名为「他为她」的运动。我向你们求助，因为我需要你们的帮助。

sentences.text: We want to end gender inequality, and to do that we need everyone to be involved.
sentences.translation: 我们想要终结性别不平等，为此我们需要每个人都参与进来。

sentences.text: This is the first campaign of its kind at the United Nations. We want to try and galvanize as many men and boys as possible to be advocates for gender equality.
sentences.translation: 这是联合国第一次发起这样的运动。我们想尝试并激励尽可能多的男性和男孩成为性别平等的倡导者。

sentences.text: And we don't just want to talk about it, but make sure it is tangible.
sentences.translation: 我们不只是想谈论它，还要确保它是切实的。

sentences.text: I was appointed six months ago as a UN Women Goodwill Ambassador, and the more I have spoken about feminism, the more I have realized that fighting for women's rights has too often become synonymous with man-hating.
sentences.translation: 六个月前我被任命为联合国妇女署亲善大使，我越多地谈论女权主义，就越意识到争取女性权利往往成了仇恨男性的代名词。

sentences.text: If there is one thing I know for certain, it is that this has to stop.
sentences.translation: 如果有一件事我确信无疑，那就是这种情况必须停止。

sentences.text: For the record, feminism by definition is the belief that men and women should have equal rights and opportunities.
sentences.translation: 郑重声明，女权主义的定义是相信男性和女性应该拥有平等的权利和机会。

sentences.text: It is the theory of the political, economic, and social equality of the sexes.
sentences.translation: 它是性别在政治、经济和社会上平等的理论。

sentences.text: I started questioning gender-based assumptions when at eight I was confused at being called bossy, because I wanted to direct the plays we would put on for our parents.
sentences.translation: 我八岁时因为想导演我们为父母表演的戏剧而被说成专横，从那时起我开始质疑基于性别的偏见。

sentences.text: But the boys were not.
sentences.translation: 但男孩们却没有被这样说。

sentences.text: When at fourteen I started being sexualized by certain elements of the press.
sentences.translation: 十四岁时，我开始被某些媒体性别化对待。

sentences.text: When at fifteen my girlfriends started dropping out of their sports teams because they didn't want to appear muscular.
sentences.translation: 十五岁时，我的女性朋友们开始退出运动队，因为她们不想显得肌肉发达。

sentences.text: When at eighteen my male friends were unable to express their feelings.
sentences.translation: 十八岁时，我的男性朋友们无法表达自己的感受。

sentences.text: I decided I was a feminist, and this seemed uncomplicated to me.
sentences.translation: 我决定我是一个女权主义者，这对我来说似乎并不复杂。

sentences.text: But my recent research has shown me that feminism has become an unpopular word.
sentences.translation: 但我最近的研究表明，女权主义已经成了一个不受欢迎的词。

sentences.text: Apparently I am among the ranks of women whose expressions are seen as too strong, too aggressive, isolating, anti-men, and unattractive.
sentences.translation: 显然我是那些表达被认为过于强烈、过于好斗、孤立、反男性和缺乏吸引力的女性之一。

sentences.text: Why is the word such an uncomfortable one?
sentences.translation: 为什么这个词让人如此不舒服？

sentences.text: I am from Britain, and I think it is right that I am paid the same as my male counterparts.
sentences.translation: 我来自英国，我认为我应该和男性同行获得同样的报酬，这是正确的。

sentences.text: I think it is right that I should be able to make decisions about my own body.
sentences.translation: 我认为我应该能够对自己的身体做出决定，这是正确的。

sentences.text: I think it is right that women be involved on my behalf in the policies and decisions that will affect my life.
sentences.translation: 我认为女性应该代表我参与将影响我生活的政策和决定，这是正确的。

sentences.text: I think it is right that socially I am afforded the same respect as men.
sentences.translation: 我认为在社会上我应该获得与男性同样的尊重，这是正确的。

sentences.text: But sadly I can say that there is no one country in the world where all women can expect to receive these rights.
sentences.translation: 但遗憾的是，我可以说世界上没有一个国家所有女性都能期望获得这些权利。

sentences.text: No country in the world can yet say they have achieved gender equality.
sentences.translation: 世界上没有一个国家可以说他们已经实现了性别平等。

sentences.text: These rights I consider to be human rights, but I am one of the lucky ones.
sentences.translation: 这些权利我认为是人权，但我是幸运的人之一。

sentences.text: My life is a sheer privilege because my parents didn't love me less because I was born a daughter.
sentences.translation: 我的生活纯粹是一种特权，因为我的父母没有因为我生为女儿而少爱我。

sentences.text: My school did not limit me because I was a girl. My mentors didn't assume I would go less far because I might give birth to a child one day.
sentences.translation: 我的学校没有因为我是女孩而限制我。我的导师们没有假设我会走得更短，因为我有一天可能会生孩子。

sentences.text: These influencers were the gender equality ambassadors that made me who I am today.
sentences.translation: 这些影响者是性别平等的大使，他们造就了今天的我。

sentences.text: They may not know it, but they are the inadvertent feminists who are changing the world today.
sentences.translation: 他们可能不知道，但他们是正在改变今天世界的无意中的女权主义者。

sentences.text: And we need more of those.
sentences.translation: 我们需要更多这样的人。

sentences.text: Men, I would like to take this opportunity to extend your formal invitation. Gender equality is your issue, too.
sentences.translation: 男士们，我想借此机会向你们发出正式邀请。性别平等也是你们的问题。

sentences.text: Because to date, I've seen my father's role as a parent being valued less by society despite my needing his presence as a child as much as my mother's.
sentences.translation: 因为到目前为止，我看到我父亲作为父母的角色被社会低估，尽管我小时候需要他的陪伴和需要母亲的一样多。

sentences.text: I have seen young men suffering from mental illness unable to ask for help for fear it would make them less of a man.
sentences.translation: 我看到年轻男性患有心理疾病却不敢寻求帮助，因为害怕这会让他们显得不够男子汉。

sentences.text: In fact, in the UK, suicide is the biggest killer of men between twenty and forty-nine.
sentences.translation: 事实上，在英国，自杀是二十到四十九岁男性的最大死因。

sentences.text: I have seen men made fragile and insecure by a distorted sense of what constitutes male success.
sentences.translation: 我看到男性因对男性成功的扭曲认知而变得脆弱和不安。

sentences.text: Men don't have the benefits of equality either.
sentences.translation: 男性也没有享受到平等的好处。

sentences.text: We don't often talk about men being imprisoned by gender stereotypes, but I can see that they are.
sentences.translation: 我们不常谈论男性被性别刻板印象所束缚，但我可以看到他们确实如此。

sentences.text: And when they are free, things will change for women as a natural consequence.
sentences.translation: 当他们获得解放时，女性的处境也会自然而然地改变。

sentences.text: Both men and women should feel free to be sensitive. Both men and women should feel free to be strong.
sentences.translation: 男性和女性都应该能自由地表达敏感。男性和女性都应该能自由地表现坚强。

sentences.text: It is time that we all perceive gender on a spectrum, not as two opposing sets of ideals.
sentences.translation: 是时候让我们所有人把性别看作一个光谱，而不是两组对立的理想。

sentences.text: If we stop defining each other by what we are not and start defining ourselves by what we are, we can all be freer.
sentences.translation: 如果我们不再用我们不是什么来定义彼此，而开始用我们是什么来定义自己，我们都可以更自由。

sentences.text: This is what HeForShe is about. It is about freedom.
sentences.translation: 这就是「他为她」的意义。它关乎自由。

sentences.text: You might be thinking, who is this Harry Potter girl and what is she doing up on stage at the UN? And it's a really good question. I have been asking myself the same thing.
sentences.translation: 你们可能在想，这个哈利·波特女孩是谁，她在联合国的舞台上做什么？这确实是个好问题。我也一直在问自己同样的问题。

sentences.text: I don't know if I am qualified to be here. All I know is that I care about this problem and I want to make it better.
sentences.translation: 我不知道我是否有资格站在这里。我只知道我关心这个问题，我想让它变得更好。

sentences.text: If not me, who? If not now, when?
sentences.translation: 如果不是我，那是谁？如果不是现在，那是什么时候？

sentences.text: I invite you to step forward, to be seen to speak up, to be the he for she. And to ask yourself if not me, who? If not now, when? Thank you.
sentences.translation: 我邀请你们站出来，让自己被看见，大声说出来，成为「他为她」。并且问问自己：如果不是我，那是谁？如果不是现在，那是什么时候？谢谢。

sentences.text: What I really want to try to achieve here is to make Mars seem possible, to make it seem as though it is something that we can do in our lifetimes.
sentences.translation: 我真正想要在这里实现的是让火星看起来是可能的，让它看起来像是我们在有生之年可以做到的事情。

sentences.text: And that you can go. Is there a way that anyone can go if they wanted to?
sentences.translation: 而且你可以去。有没有一种方式让任何想去的人都可以去？

sentences.text: There are really two fundamental paths. One is that we stay on Earth forever and then there will be an eventual extinction event.
sentences.translation: 确实只有两条根本路径。一条是我们永远留在地球上，然后终将面临灭绝事件。

sentences.text: The alternative is to become a spacefaring civilization and a multi-planet species.
sentences.translation: 另一条是成为一个太空文明和多星球物种。

sentences.text: You want to wake up in the morning and think the future is going to be great, and that's what being a spacefaring civilization is all about.
sentences.translation: 你想每天早上醒来时觉得未来会很美好，而这就是成为太空文明的意义所在。

sentences.text: It's about believing in the future and thinking that the future will be better than the past.
sentences.translation: 这关乎相信未来，相信未来会比过去更好。

sentences.text: And I can't think of anything more exciting than going out there and being among the stars.
sentences.translation: 我想不出比走出去、置身于群星之间更令人兴奋的事情了。

sentences.text: The first journey to Mars is going to be really very dangerous. The risk of fatality will be high. There is just no way around it.
sentences.translation: 第一次火星之旅将非常危险。死亡风险很高。这是无法回避的。

sentences.text: It would be basically, are you prepared to die? If that's okay, then you're a candidate for going.
sentences.translation: 基本上就是，你准备好面对死亡了吗？如果可以的话，那你就是候选人。

sentences.text: We need to figure out how to create a self-sustaining city on Mars.
sentences.translation: 我们需要弄清楚如何在火星上建造一座自给自足的城市。

sentences.text: The critical threshold, I think, is to build a city that is self-sustaining enough that it can survive even if the supply ships from Earth stop coming.
sentences.translation: 我认为关键门槛是建造一座足够自给自足的城市，即使地球的补给船停止来了它也能生存。

sentences.text: If you can get to that point, then the probability of civilization lasting for a very long time is high.
sentences.translation: 如果你能达到那个点，那么文明持续很长时间的概率就很高。

sentences.text: The goal would be to get the cost of moving to Mars to be roughly equivalent to the median house price in the United States.
sentences.translation: 目标是将搬到火星的费用降低到大约相当于美国房价中位数的水平。

sentences.text: So about two hundred thousand dollars. Then I think the probability of establishing a self-sustaining civilization is very high.
sentences.translation: 大约二十万美元。那样的话，我认为建立一个自给自足的文明的概率非常高。

sentences.text: Full reusability is really the key to making this affordable. If aircraft were not reusable, very few people would fly.
sentences.translation: 完全可重复使用是使其经济可行的真正关键。如果飞机不能重复使用，很少有人会坐飞机。

sentences.text: A fully reusable rocket is the Holy Grail of rocketry. No one has ever done it.
sentences.translation: 完全可重复使用的火箭是火箭技术的圣杯。从来没有人做到过。

sentences.text: But I think it can be done, and I think SpaceX is going to try to do it.
sentences.translation: 但我认为这可以做到，而且我认为SpaceX将会尝试做到。

sentences.text: The rocket booster comes back and lands on the launch pad. Think about how crazy that is. That is really the fundamental breakthrough that's needed.
sentences.translation: 火箭助推器返回并降落在发射台上。想想这有多疯狂。这确实是所需的根本性突破。

sentences.text: It has to be something that is exciting and inspiring. Life cannot just be about solving one miserable problem after another.
sentences.translation: 它必须是令人兴奋和鼓舞人心的。生活不能只是解决一个又一个痛苦的问题。

sentences.text: There needs to be things that make you glad to be alive.
sentences.translation: 需要有让你很高兴自己活着的事情。

sentences.text: The thing that got me started on this was, I just kept wondering, why aren't we making progress toward getting to Mars?
sentences.translation: 让我开始做这件事的是，我一直在想，为什么我们在登陆火星方面没有取得进展？

sentences.text: I went to the NASA website to find out when we were going to Mars, and there was nothing.
sentences.translation: 我去NASA网站查找我们什么时候去火星，结果什么都没有。

sentences.text: So initially my thought was, well, perhaps I can just shift the budget allocation at NASA a little bit.
sentences.translation: 所以最初我的想法是，也许我可以稍微调整一下NASA的预算分配。

sentences.text: People sometimes think technology just automatically improves every year, but it actually doesn't.
sentences.translation: 人们有时认为技术每年都会自动进步，但实际上并非如此。

sentences.text: It only improves if smart people work very hard to make it better.
sentences.translation: 只有聪明人非常努力地工作，技术才会进步。

sentences.text: By itself technology, if people don't work on it, it will actually decline.
sentences.translation: 如果没有人去努力，技术本身实际上会衰退。

sentences.text: Look at ancient Egypt. They built the pyramids and then they forgot how to build the pyramids. And then the Romans built these incredible aqueducts and then they forgot how to do that.
sentences.translation: 看看古埃及。他们建造了金字塔，然后他们忘记了如何建造金字塔。然后罗马人建造了那些令人难以置信的水渠，然后他们也忘记了如何建造。

sentences.text: We should try very hard to become a multi-planet species. That's what the future should look like.
sentences.translation: 我们应该非常努力地成为一个多星球物种。这就是未来应该的样子。

sentences.text: I want to make something that inspires people, even if they never go to Mars.
sentences.translation: 我想创造一些能激励人们的东西，即使他们永远不会去火星。

sentences.text: The key is making it affordable for almost anyone who wants to go.
sentences.translation: 关键是让几乎任何想去的人都能负担得起。

sentences.text: History is going to bifurcate along two directions. One path is we become a multiplanet species, and the other is we remain on one planet until some eventual extinction event.
sentences.translation: 历史将沿着两个方向分叉。一条路是我们成为多星球物种，另一条是我们留在一个星球上直到某个最终的灭绝事件。

sentences.text: I say let us choose the path that leads to the stars. Let us become the makers of our own fate.
sentences.translation: 我说让我们选择通往星辰的道路。让我们成为自己命运的创造者。

sentences.text: If we can land people on Mars within the next ten to fifteen years, that would be an incredible achievement for humanity.
sentences.translation: 如果我们能在未来十到十五年内将人送上火星，那将是人类一项不可思议的成就。

sentences.text: Mars is the next logical step. It has a day roughly the same length as Earth. It has resources that can be used to sustain life.
sentences.translation: 火星是下一个合乎逻辑的步骤。它的一天和地球的大致相同。它有可以用来维持生命的资源。

sentences.text: With the right technology and the right level of determination, we can create a new branch of human civilization on Mars.
sentences.translation: 有了正确的技术和足够的决心，我们可以在火星上创造人类文明的新分支。

sentences.text: I believe this is something worth dedicating our lives to. Making life multiplanetary.
sentences.translation: 我相信这是值得我们毕生奉献的事业。让生命成为多星球的。

sentences.text: Thank you, President Tessier-Lavigne, Provost Drell, deans, and all of the faculty and staff.
sentences.translation: 感谢泰西耶-拉维涅校长、德雷尔教务长、各位院长以及全体教职员工。

sentences.text: It is such an honor to be here today. Stanford and Apple have been neighbors for a long time.
sentences.translation: 今天能来到这里，深感荣幸。斯坦福和苹果一直是邻居。

sentences.text: Today's ceremony brings to mind a similar event fourteen years ago, and the memorable speech by Steve Jobs.
sentences.translation: 今天的典礼让我想起十四年前的一个类似活动，以及史蒂夫·乔布斯那篇令人难忘的演讲。

sentences.text: Steve's vision for Apple was a company that turned powerful technology into tools that were easy to use, tools that would help people realize their dreams.
sentences.translation: 史蒂夫对苹果的愿景是一家把强大技术变成易用工具的公司，帮助人们实现梦想的工具。

sentences.text: Technology can be and should be a force for good. That's been the North Star for me and for the company I've been privileged to lead.
sentences.translation: 技术可以而且应该是一股向善的力量。这一直是我和我有幸领导的公司的北极星。

sentences.text: In a world where we spend so much time on our devices, I know I am asking you to be a bit countercultural.
sentences.translation: 在一个我们花太多时间在设备上的世界里，我知道我是在要求你们有些反潮流。

sentences.text: But for every problem technology can create, there is a solution that technology can provide.
sentences.translation: 但对于技术创造的每一个问题，都有一个技术可以提供的解决方案。

sentences.text: In recent years, the idea that tech companies should be immune from responsibility has been blown to pieces.
sentences.translation: 近年来，科技公司应该免于责任的想法已经被粉碎了。

sentences.text: Too many seem to think that good intentions excuse away harmful outcomes.
sentences.translation: 太多人似乎认为良好的意图可以为有害的结果开脱。

sentences.text: But whether you like it or not, what you build and what you create define who you are.
sentences.translation: 但不管你喜欢与否，你建造的和创造的定义了你是谁。

sentences.text: It feels a bit crazy that anyone should have to say this, but if you've built a chaos factory, you can't dodge responsibility for the chaos.
sentences.translation: 说这话感觉有点疯狂，但如果你建造了一个混乱工厂，你就不能逃避对混乱的责任。

sentences.text: Taking responsibility means having the courage to think things through.
sentences.translation: 承担责任意味着有勇气把事情想清楚。

sentences.text: If you want to take credit, first learn to take responsibility.
sentences.translation: 如果你想获得荣誉，首先学会承担责任。

sentences.text: If we accept as normal and unavoidable that everything in our lives can be aggregated and sold, we lose the freedom to be human.
sentences.translation: 如果我们把生活中的一切都可以被汇总和出售当作正常和不可避免的，我们就失去了做人的自由。

sentences.text: In a world without digital privacy, even if you have done nothing wrong other than think differently, you begin to censor yourself.
sentences.translation: 在一个没有数字隐私的世界里，即使你除了想法不同之外没有做错什么，你也会开始自我审查。

sentences.text: Not everything that is faced can be changed. But nothing can be changed until it's faced.
sentences.translation: 不是所有面对的事情都能改变。但不去面对就什么也改变不了。

sentences.text: Technology is capable of being the best and worst of us. It can bring out the worst in people and can amplify our worst impulses.
sentences.translation: 技术能展现我们最好和最坏的一面。它可以激发人们最坏的一面，放大我们最坏的冲动。

sentences.text: It can deepen divisions, incite violence, and even undermine our shared sense of what is true and what is false.
sentences.translation: 它可以加深分裂，煽动暴力，甚至破坏我们对真假的共同认知。

sentences.text: This is a crisis. A crisis of our own making. One that is real. And it is not imagined, or exaggerated, or crazy.
sentences.translation: 这是一场危机。一场我们自己造成的危机。一场真实的危机。它不是想象的，不是夸大的，也不是疯狂的。

sentences.text: At Apple, we reject the excuse that getting the most out of technology means trading away your right to privacy.
sentences.translation: 在苹果，我们拒绝接受这样的借口：要最大限度地利用技术就意味着要出卖你的隐私权。

sentences.text: So graduates, whatever you do with your life, be the builder.
sentences.translation: 所以毕业生们，无论你的人生做什么，做建设者。

sentences.text: You don't have to build a rocket ship or a social network or an app. What you build doesn't have to be an invention.
sentences.translation: 你不必建造火箭或社交网络或应用程序。你建造的不一定是一项发明。

sentences.text: What you build can be a community, an institution, a life, a family.
sentences.translation: 你建造的可以是一个社区、一个机构、一种生活、一个家庭。

sentences.text: Whatever you do, don't just do it for what you'll get out of it. That is a recipe for misery.
sentences.translation: 无论你做什么，不要仅仅为了从中获得什么而做。那是痛苦的配方。

sentences.text: Find the intersection of what you love and what you can do for others. That is where you will find meaning.
sentences.translation: 找到你所热爱的和你能为他人做的事情的交汇点。那就是你会找到意义的地方。

sentences.text: Be builders. Be the people who build something more significant than themselves.
sentences.translation: 做建设者。做那些建造比自己更重要的东西的人。

sentences.text: There are problems that need you. Injustices that need your outrage. Take the values you hold dear and put them into practice.
sentences.translation: 有些问题需要你们。有些不公正需要你们的愤怒。把你们珍视的价值观付诸实践。

sentences.text: You don't have to choose between doing good and doing well. It's a false choice, today more than ever.
sentences.translation: 你不必在做好事和做好自己之间选择。这是一个错误的选择，今天比以往任何时候都是。

sentences.text: My generation has failed you in some significant ways. We spent too much time debating. We have been too focused on the fight and not enough on the things worth fighting for.
sentences.translation: 我这一代人在某些重要方面辜负了你们。我们花了太多时间争论。我们过于关注战斗本身，而不够关注值得为之战斗的事情。

sentences.text: If you find yourself spending more time fighting than building, stop and recalibrate.
sentences.translation: 如果你发现自己花在战斗上的时间比建设上的多，请停下来重新校准。

sentences.text: In the end, building is how we make progress.
sentences.translation: 最终，建设是我们取得进步的方式。

sentences.text: Climate change is real and urgent. And it is up to all of us to fight for the planet we share.
sentences.translation: 气候变化是真实而紧迫的。我们所有人都有责任为我们共同的星球而战。

sentences.text: Don't let anyone tell you the problems are too big or too hard to solve.
sentences.translation: 不要让任何人告诉你问题太大或太难解决。

sentences.text: Every great problem was once a problem that nobody thought they could solve. And then somebody did.
sentences.translation: 每一个重大问题都曾经是一个没有人认为自己能解决的问题。然后有人解决了。

sentences.text: Fourteen years ago Steve stood on this stage and told your predecessors to stay hungry, stay foolish.
sentences.translation: 十四年前史蒂夫站在这个舞台上，告诉你们的前辈们求知若饥、虚怀若愚。

sentences.text: I have always taken great comfort in those words and I hope you will too.
sentences.translation: 这些话一直给我巨大的安慰，我希望它们也能给你们安慰。

sentences.text: Today, I want to add to Steve's advice. Be the last to accept that the way things are is the way they have to be.
sentences.translation: 今天，我想在史蒂夫的建议上补充一点。做最后一个接受事情就是这样而不能改变的人。

sentences.text: Be the first to stand up and say something has to change. Be the first to make it better.
sentences.translation: 做第一个站起来说必须改变的人。做第一个让它变得更好的人。

sentences.text: Build a better world. You have the talent, the resources, and the opportunity. Now go make it happen.
sentences.translation: 建设一个更好的世界。你们有才华、有资源、有机会。现在就去实现它。

sentences.text: Congratulations, Class of 2019. Get out there and build the future. The world needs your energy, your passion, and your impatience for progress.
sentences.translation: 祝贺2019届毕业生。走出去，建设未来。世界需要你们的活力、热情和对进步的迫不及待。
